summary: Extraction-fidelity review of the proposed DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and coverage-assessment records found the candidate's abstract-located sample, dosing, timing, endpoint, and effect wording consistent with the cited PubMed metadata snapshots and source records. The records preserve the abstract-extracted maturity boundary and retain caveats for missing full-text/table reconciliation, so this lane has no open major or critical findings.

findings.claim_or_issue: The DKD study, outcome, result, and finding records extract N=9, oral dasatinib 100 mg plus quercetin 1000 mg for 3 days, pre/post tissue and blood collection 11 days after completion, and directional senescent-cell/SASP biomarker decreases from PMID 31542391.
findings.why_it_matters: These values are the central exact-effect repair for the DKD human biomarker slice, and the records avoid adding unsupported numeric biomarker effect sizes.
findings.recommended_action: No change required for this lane; keep the full-text numeric biomarker table extraction as a future coverage action rather than a blocker for the abstract-level repair.

findings.claim_or_issue: The IPF randomized-pilot records extract N=12, 1:1 randomization, dasatinib 100 mg/day plus quercetin 1250 mg/day for 3 consecutive days per week over 3 weeks, 108/108 scheduled doses, 60/60 planned assessments, and no meaningful between-arm clinical-measure difference wording from PMID 36857968.
findings.why_it_matters: The new dosing-completion outcome/result corrects the operational exact-effect gap without turning the pilot feasibility result into an efficacy claim.
findings.recommended_action: No change required for this lane; keep the legacy generic IPF outcome/result reconciliation as a separate extraction refresh task.

findings.claim_or_issue: The AD-risk study, outcome, result, and finding records extract the D+Q schedule, 12 completers, MoCA +1.0 points with 95% CI -0.7 to 2.7, lowest-baseline-MoCA subgroup +2.0 points with 95% CI 0.1 to 4.0, TNF-alpha -3.0% with 95% CI -13.0 to 7.1, and inverse TNF-alpha/MoCA correlation r=-0.65, p=0.02 from PMID 40010154.
findings.why_it_matters: The extracted effect values are exactly the abstract-reported estimates and the candidate keeps them in an exploratory, low-confidence single-arm interpretation.
findings.recommended_action: No change required for this lane; mobility endpoint values should remain deferred until full-article extraction.

findings.claim_or_issue: The candidate records use maturity_status abstract_extracted for the new DKD, IPF, and AD-risk exact-effect records and the coverage assessment explicitly keeps durable full-text/table reconciliation gaps open.
findings.why_it_matters: This prevents abstract-located repairs from being mistaken for source-snapshot-backed full-text extraction or cross-human synthesis readiness.
findings.recommended_action: No change required for this lane; coordinator post-run export and later extraction-refresh work can carry the remaining gaps.
